expectations.text: asks-clarification: Output asks the user clarifying questions rather than generating a full journey map
expectations.evidence: The output generates a full 8-stage journey map (Awareness through Advocacy) with detailed tables for each stage. While the note at the top states 'No specific application details were provided. This is a generic journey map template' and the Summary of Key Gaps section asks for inputs, the output does not stop to ask clarifying questions — it proceeds to generate the full map.

expectations.text: no-invented-product: Output does NOT invent a product concept — no fake app names, no assumed features
expectations.evidence: The output invents a generic app concept with assumed features and stages: app store browsing, sign-up screens, tutorials, push notifications, referral programs, in-app support chat, and social media sharing. While no specific app name is used, the journey assumes a consumer mobile app with standard features like reviews, settings, notifications, and community features.

expectations.text: asks-about-product: Clarifying questions include what the app/product does
expectations.evidence: The Summary of Key Gaps section includes: '1. Define the app -- What does it do? Who is it for?'

expectations.text: asks-about-users: Clarifying questions include who the target users are
expectations.evidence: The Summary of Key Gaps section includes: '2. Define the persona -- Demographics, goals, frustrations, tech comfort level.'
